geographicSummary: District Eight covers the South and Far South sides of Columbus, from the Scioto River on the west to Big Walnut Park on the east, and south toward the Obetz and Groveport borders. The district has the largest Black population among the nine districts and borders the communities of Lockbourne and Groveport.

pointsOfInterest.name: Scioto Southland Community Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Modern community center with weight room, boxing room, gymnasiums, kitchen, and classrooms.

pointsOfInterest.name: Marion-Franklin Recreation Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Community center serving Far South Columbus with programs, fitness facilities, and meeting spaces.

pointsOfInterest.name: Rickenbacker Airport and Logistics Hub
pointsOfInterest.description: Cargo-focused airport and surrounding logistics complex, a major employment center for the district.

pointsOfInterest.name: Glenwood Community Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Recreation facility with swimming, fitness programs, food pantry services, and youth activities.

pointsOfInterest.name: Three Creeks Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 1,067-acre park at the confluence of Alum, Big Walnut, and Blacklick Creeks with trails and wetlands.

pointsOfInterest.name: Obetz Zettler Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Park and event venue hosting the annual Obetz Zucchinifest and community recreation.

areaCommissions.name: South Side Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Third Tuesday of each month, 6:30 PM
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Columbus Metropolitan Library — Parsons Branch, 1113 Parsons Ave.

data.data_note: Figures from original site launch. Block-group weighted aggregation to council districts.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (build_district_profiles.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
